target.title: Add accessible Workboard movement controls
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a compact writable-only Workboard card status control that moves cards through the existing `workboard.cards.move` path.
- Support keyboard movement with the native status menu plus focused `ArrowLeft` / `ArrowRight` shortcuts, while preserving drag/drop as the pointer enhancement.
- Cover read-only suppression, select-change movement, keyboard-arrow movement, and update the Workboard workflow docs.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/views/workboard.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/styles/workboard.css docs/plugins/workboard.md`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Playwright browser proof on the mock Control UI: Tabbed to the Workboard status control, pressed `ArrowRight`, observed `workboard.cards.move` with `status: "scheduled"`, reloaded, and confirmed the card remained visible under Scheduled.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Keyboard-only Workboard operators can move a card to another status without opening a modal; read-only operators still cannot mutate cards.
Real environment tested: Local Vite Control UI with the repo mock Gateway in Playwright Chromium.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Loaded `/workboard`, tabbed to `.workboard-card__move-select`, pressed `ArrowRight`, waited for `workboard.cards.move`, reloaded with the moved card returned by `workboard.cards.list`.
Evidence after fix: The mock Gateway recorded `{ "id": "card-keyboard-proof", "status": "scheduled", "position": 1000 }`; after reload, the card rendered in the Scheduled column.
Observed result after fix: The movement persisted through the Workboard move endpoint and remained visible after refresh.
What was not tested: Full production Gateway persistence beyond the existing `workboard.cards.move` contract; the browser proof used the repo mock Gateway.
